label: Dense vector retrieval

node.label: Dense vector retrieval
node.definition: Dense retrieval embeds queries and candidates into vector space and finds approximate nearest neighbors under a learned similarity function.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Dense vector retrieval is modeled as depending on External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Hybrid retrieval & reranking, Vector databases & ANN indexes, Retrieval-augmented generation.
examples: atlas downstream link to Hybrid retrieval & reranking
atlas downstream link to Vector databases & ANN indexes
atlas downstream link to Retrieval-augmented generation
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Dense vector retrieval has a `uses` dependency on External retrieval & ranking because External retrieval & ranking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Dense vector retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Embeddings & distributed representations.

In this atlas graph, Dense vector retrieval has a `uses` dependency on Contrastive and mutual-information objectives because Contrastive and mutual-information objectives is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
